ORDER REGARDING SPECIALLY APPEARANCE DEFENDANT SAKS INCORPORATED'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT FOR LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SAKS INCORPORATED MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT FOR LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION. NO APPEARANCES, THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: SAKS INCORPORATED'S MOTION TO QUASH IS GRANTED. THIS MOTION TO QUASH ORIGINALLY CAME ON FOR HEARING ON FEBRUARY 16, 2021. ON MARCH 3, 2021, THE COURT ISSUED AN ORDER EXPLAINING THAT PLAINTIFF HAD NOT MET HIS BURDEN OF DEMONSTRATING SUFFICIENT MINIMUM CONTACTS FOR GENERAL OR SPECIFIC JURISDICTION. THE COURT CONTINUED THE MOTION TO ALLOW PLAINTIFF TO CONDUCT LIMITED JURISDICTIONAL DISCOVERY. SAKS INCORPORATED'S SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF EXPLAINS THAT NO SUCH DISCOVERY OCCURRED AND PLAINTIFF HAS NOT FILED ANY SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEFS OR EVIDENCE. THEREFORE, PLAINTIFF STILL FAILS TO MEET HIS JURISDICTIONAL BURDEN AND THE MOTION IS GRANTED. ORDER SIGNED. (SEE MINI-MINUTES OR ORDER SIGNED THIS DATE FOR THE COMPLETE DETAILS OF THE COURT'S RULING) JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; NOT REPORTED. (302/EPS)

Off Calendar Jun-18-2021 Set For Order To Show Cause On Aug-24-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Specially Appearing Defendant Saks Incorporated Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
Off Calendar May-18-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT SAKS INCORPORATED Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction.

No appearances, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Saks Incorporated's motion to quash is granted. This motion to quash originally came on for hearing on February 16, 2021. On March 3, 2021, the Court issued an order explaining that plaintiff had not met his burden of demonstrating sufficient minimum contacts for general or specific jurisdiction. (See Ziller Electronics Lab GmbH v. Superior Court (1988) 206 Cal.App.3d 1222, 1232-1233; BBA Aviation PLC v. Superior Court (2010) 190 Cal.App.4th 421, 428-429 ["The plaintiff must do more than merely allege jurisdictional facts; plaintiff must provide affidavits and other authenticated documents demonstrating competent evidence of jurisdictional facts."].) The record indicated that Saks Incorporated may be some sort of holding company or had a limited role in processing plaintiff's payroll. There was insufficient evidence that Saks Incorporated pervasively controlled plaintiff's purported employer (Saks & Co. LLC), who has already answered the complaint, or that Saks Incorporated had a role in the employment decisions that form the basis of this case. The Court continued the motion to allow plaintiff to conduct limited jurisdictional discovery. Saks Incorporated's supplemental brief explains that no such discovery occurred and plaintiff has not filed any supplemental briefs or evidence. Therefore, plaintiff still fails to meet his jurisdictional burden and the motion is granted.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Not Reported. (302/EPS)
